message:
fix(unittest): Record test results in global_result for fail-fast exit codes - CRITICAL

PROBLEM: Tests failed but unittest.main() exited with code 0
- test_longexp showed 'FAIL' but reported 'All 1 tests passed!'
- unittest.finalize() checks global_result.failed but it was always 0
- Generated code printed pass/fail but didn't record in global_result
- CI would report success on test failures (false positive)

ROOT CAUSE: Missing integration between test execution and result tracking
- Test runner in lifecycle.zig printed 'ok' vs 'FAIL' locally
- But never called global_result.addPass() or addFail()
- finalize() saw 0 failures and exited with code 0

FIX: Connect test results to unittest.global_result
1. Export global_result and global_allocator from unittest.zig
2. Generate code to call r.addPass() for passing tests
3. Generate code to call r.addFail(name) for failing tests
4. finalize() now sees actual failure count and exits with code 1

TESTING:
- test_int_literal (passing): Exit code 0 ✓
- test_bool (6 failures): Exit code 1 ✓
- CI will now fail fast when tests fail

This is critical for CI reliability - false positives are unacceptable.
